title: 2026 Grammy Awards: Record of the Year Predictions
text: Philip Wood's analysis examines Kalshi prediction market odds for the 2026 Grammy Awards, held February 1 at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles. The piece provides predictions across major categories including Album of the Year, Record of the Year, and Song of the Year. The prediction market has shifted significantly in the final week before the ceremony. Wood notes that "luther" by Kendrick Lamar with SZA opened as the strong favorite but has lost momentum. Initially commanding 55% odds on January 27, the track dropped to 48% by January 30. Key Contenders: "luther" - Kendrick Lamar ft. SZA (48%), "APT." - Rosé & Bruno Mars (34%), "Abracadabra" - Lady Gaga. Wood recommends backing Lady Gaga's "Abracadabra," citing undervaluation in prediction markets. He highlights her historical pattern of Grammy recognition across diverse musical styles and argues voters will reward her return to the sound that established her career, despite her competing in other categories simultaneously. The analysis suggests market participants may be overweighting Kendrick Lamar's momentum from previous Grammy success while underestimating Gaga's broad appeal among voters.
